title: String Operations Should Not Rely on the Default System Locale
description: This rule warns when string operations such as toLowerCase(), toUpperCase() or format() rely on the default system locale. This could create problems with international characters or number representations. For example, the Turkish language converts the lower case 'i' to an uppercase 'I' with a dot over it. These issues may be hard to reproduce and fix. So, it's recommended to always specify a locale for these operations.
recommendation: Always specify a locale when calling the methods toLowerCase(), toUpperCase() or format() on string objects. For example, myString.toLowerCase(Locale.TR).
impact: Locale-sensitive bugs can be nearly, if not completely, impossible to reproduce when it's time to fix them. Such bugs can also lead to unexpected behavior with international characters or number representations.
